meaning: n.工作服的上部,围兜,口水兜v.(使)过多喝酒
examples: I show up on time, but I don't have my running shoes, or another critical item, like my race bib.
Madeleine showered and put on her first spring dress: an apple-green baby-doll dress with a bib collar and a high hem.
Ok, take off the bib. Go to play.
Get off the chair, ski down, find a new bib of the opposite color, rinse, repeat.
In 1985, President Reagan named Mr. Forbes Chairman of the bi-partisan Board for International Broadcasting (BIB).
